# JOS-S31 — ID Naming Convention Standard

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)** for new IDs minted on/after 2026-05-16.

## 1. Purpose

Every governed component has a human-readable ID (the prefix + suffix on
its filename, the `id:` in its frontmatter). This standard defines the
**shape** of that ID per component type, with the rule: when a semantic
slot exists, the ID MUST embed it.

## 2. The Decision: Intuitive vs Sequential

**Intuitive** (slot-embedded): the ID names the semantic position of
the object in a known taxonomy.

**Sequential** (numeric): the ID is the next integer in the type's
counter, with no semantic content.

The decision rule:

```
IF component_type.has_finite_semantic_taxonomy:
  ID = intuitive (slot-embedded)
ELSE:
  ID = sequential
```

A taxonomy is "finite + semantic" when:

- The number of valid slot values is bounded (not open-ended)
- A reader who knows JOS can name the slot values without lookup
- Each slot value has a stable, distinct meaning

## 3. Per-Component-Type Patterns

### 3.1 Intuitive (slot-embedded) types

| Component | Taxonomy | Pattern | Examples |
|---|---|---|---|
| Method per governance domain | 9 domains | `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-<DOMAIN>` | `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENG`, `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-DOC` |
| Method per doc-type concern | 11 doc-types | `JOS-METHOD-DOC-TYPE-<NAME>` | `JOS-METHOD-DOC-TYPE-STANDARDIZATION` |
| Method (synthesis / meta-pattern) | the META slot | `JOS-METHOD-META` | `JOS-METHOD-META` |
| Doc-type standard *(target form, legacy in JOS-S19..S30)* | 11 doc-types | `JOS-S-DOC-<NAME>` | `JOS-S-DOC-OVERVIEW` (migration tracked) |
| Layer-scoped artifact *(target)* | 3 layers | `JOS-<TYPE>-LAYER-<NAME>` | `JOS-METHOD-LAYER-KNOWLEDGE` (if/when minted) |
| Boundary-contract-slot artifact *(target)* | 5 slots | `JOS-<TYPE>-SLOT-<NAME>` | `JOS-METHOD-SLOT-INTERFACE` (if/when minted) |

### 3.2 Sequential types (no semantic taxonomy)

| Component | Pattern | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Decision (`JOS-D####`) | `JOS-D0001`, `JOS-D0055` | Decisions accrete over time; no categorical key |
| Rule (`JOS-R##`) | `JOS-R01`, `JOS-R19` | Rules accrete; some span multiple domains |
| Standard (`JOS-S##`) | `JOS-S01`, `JOS-S31` | Standards accrete across many topics |
| SOP (`JOS-SOP-###`) | `JOS-SOP-001`, `JOS-SOP-014` | Procedures accrete; topic too varied for a slot |
| Principle (`JOS-P###`) | `JOS-P24` | Principles accrete from doctrine work |
| Concept (`JOS-C###`) | `JOS-C45` | Concepts accrete from framework work |
| Standing Order (`JOS-SO-###`) | `JOS-SO-012` | Same as SOPs |
| Spec (`JOS-SPEC-###`) | `JOS-SPEC-012` | Specs accrete by problem area, no fixed slot |
| Guide (`JOS-GD-###`) | `JOS-GD-002` | Same as SPEC |
| Playbook (`JOS-PB-###`) | `JOS-PB-006` | Same as SPEC |
| Gate (`JOS-GATE-###`) | `JOS-GATE-009` | Same as SPEC |

Sequential IDs are still required to ship with a **descriptive slug
in the filename** per JOS-S18 §2.2 (`JOS-R19-intuitive-id-naming.md`).
The slug is the discoverability layer for sequential IDs.

## 4. Intuitive ID Structure Rules

When an ID embeds a slot, follow these structural rules:

### 4.1 Separator

Use hyphens (`-`) between segments. Match the JOS-S18 kebab-case file
naming convention.

### 4.2 Casing

ID segments are **UPPER-KEBAB**: `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENG`, not
`jos-method-domain-eng` and not `JOS-Method-Domain-Eng`. The all-caps
form is the canonical written form (frontmatter `id:`, registry entries,
in-prose references). Filenames lowercase the slug per S18 §2.1:
`JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENG-engineering-domain-method.md`.

### 4.3 Segment order (specific → general → none)

The pattern is: `JOS` (org prefix) → `<TYPE>` (component type) →
`<SLOT-DIMENSION>` (what taxonomy) → `<SLOT-VALUE>` (which slot).

Example: `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENG` reads as: "the JOS Method that
governs the ENG slot of the DOMAIN dimension."

### 4.4 Slot-dimension prefix is required when ambiguous

Domain methods MUST use `DOMAIN-` (not just `JOS-METHOD-ENG`) because:

- A future Method could be slotted by Layer (`JOS-METHOD-LAYER-KNOWLEDGE`)
- Or by Boundary Contract slot (`JOS-METHOD-SLOT-INTERFACE`)
- Or by Doc-type (`JOS-METHOD-DOC-TYPE-STANDARDIZATION`)

Without the dimension prefix, two Methods sharing a slot-value letter
collide.

### 4.5 Slot values use the canonical FULL name (no abbreviations)

**Abbreviations are forbidden in slot values.** The whole point of
intuitive naming (R19) is that a reader recognizes the slot without
dereferencing. An abbreviation (`ENG`, `DOC`, `FIN`) breaks that — it
forces every reader to look up which `ENG` is meant (engineering?
English? engagement?). Use the full word, every time.

Acceptable trade-off: **a slot value that is already a full word in
common usage** can stay in its canonical short form. The only
governance-domain slot value where this applies is `META` (META is
the full word for the meta-governance domain — it's not an
abbreviation of anything longer).

For governance domains: use the 10-domain full-name list (§5).

For doc-types: use the standardized name from the JOS-D0054 table
(`OVERVIEW, GUARANTEES, WDGLL, HEALTH-STANDARD, …`). These are
already full words; no expansion needed.

For layers: `KNOWLEDGE, GOVERNANCE, ACTION`.

For boundary contract slots: `IDENTITY, GOVERNANCE, CAPABILITY,
INTERFACE, LIFECYCLE`.

For glossary domain prefixes (per JOS-D0056 federated glossary):
the full-name pattern applies — `governance:rule`, `engineering:repository`,
`business:archetype`, etc. — never `gov:rule` or `eng:repository`.

## 5. The 10 Governance Domains (canonical FULL names)

This is the canonical slot-value list for `DOMAIN-` IDs. All values
are full words — no abbreviations.

| Slot value | Domain | Brief |
|---|---|---|
| `META` | Meta | Governance-of-governance (JOS itself, frameworks, the holonic structure). `META` is the full word, not an abbreviation. |
| `ENGINEERING` | Engineering | Code, infrastructure, deployment, technical architecture |
| `DOCUMENTATION` | Documentation | Doc-types, declaration surfaces, written artifacts |
| `DATA` | Data | Storage, schemas, retention, lineage |
| `FINANCE` | Finance | Budgets, billing, cost attribution |
| `STRATEGY` | Strategy | Roadmaps, prioritization, market direction |
| `OPERATIONS` | Operations | Daily run, incidents, ops procedures |
| `PEOPLE` | People | Roles, archetypes, onboarding, conduct |
| `LEGAL` | Legal | Compliance, IP, contracts |
| `BUSINESS` | Business (Business Operations) | Cross-functional business-operations vocabulary — value streams, departments, decision rights, capabilities-as-disciplines, role archetypes. House for World-Standard-class content. Added 2026-05-16 via JOS-D0056. |

This list is locked. New domains require a Decision + this section
update. **Lowercase prefix forms** (for glossary federation per JOS-D0056):
`meta:`, `engineering:`, `documentation:`, `data:`, `finance:`,
`strategy:`, `operations:`, `people:`, `legal:`, `business:`, plus the
cross-cutting `core:` namespace.

## 6. The 11 Doc-Types (canonical short codes)

This is the canonical slot-value list for `DOC-TYPE-` IDs, taken from
JOS-D0054:

`OVERVIEW, GUARANTEES, WDGLL, HEALTH-STANDARD, PURPOSE, ARCHITECTURE,
README, DECISION-LOG, VISION, ROADMAP, CHANGELOG`

## 7. The 5 Boundary Contract Slots (canonical short codes)

Per JOS-D0054:

`IDENTITY, GOVERNANCE, CAPABILITY, INTERFACE, LIFECYCLE`

## 8. The 3 Architectural Layers (canonical short codes)

Per JOS-D0053:

`KNOWLEDGE, GOVERNANCE, ACTION`

## 9. Filename Convention for Intuitive IDs

For intuitive IDs, the filename pattern is:

`{ID}-{descriptive-slug}.md`

The descriptive slug helps when scanning a directory listing and
distinguishes the file from the bare ID in URLs/links. Examples:

- `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENG-engineering-domain-method.md`
- `JOS-METHOD-DOC-TYPE-STANDARDIZATION-doc-type-standardization-method.md`
- `JOS-METHOD-META-meta-synthesis-method.md`

Filenames remain lowercase kebab per JOS-S18 §2.1; the **ID portion of
the filename keeps the canonical UPPER-KEBAB form** (it's literally the
ID, copy-pasted). This is the one exception to S18's "all lowercase"
guideline and is anchored here.

## 10. Migration

Legacy sequential IDs in types that should have been intuitive
(currently: `JOS-METHOD-001`) migrate via JOS-SOP-014. UUID preserved.
Old ID kept resolvable during the coexistence window.

Migration is tracked under JOSW2 scope 670 Phase 3 (Method authoring +
naming convention rollout).

## 11. Anti-patterns

- **Abbreviated slot values** (`JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENG`, `governance:` →
  `gov:`, `engineering:` → `eng:`) — forbidden as of v1.1. Intuitive
  naming means full words. The only exception is when the short form
  IS the full word in common usage (`META` for the meta-governance
  domain).
- **Embedding the slot value without the dimension prefix** (`JOS-METHOD-ENGINEERING`
  instead of `JOS-METHOD-DOMAIN-ENGINEERING`) — collides with future
  slot-dimensions
- **Sequential ID for a type with a clear taxonomy** (`JOS-METHOD-003`
  for a domain method) — forbidden by R19
- **Mixed casing in IDs** (`JOS-Method-Domain-Engineering`) — IDs are UPPER-KEBAB
- **Slug-only filenames for governance objects** (`engineering-domain-method.md`)
  — filename MUST start with the ID for directory-scan discoverability
- **Acronym prefixes in glossary federation** (`gov:rule` instead of
  `governance:rule`, `bus:archetype` instead of `business:archetype`)
  — same rule applies: full words, not 3-letter codes

## 12. Why required-flexibility

Naming is one of the cheapest things to get right at mint time and
one of the most expensive things to fix later. Every cross-reference,
every cached URL, every agent's mental model carries the old name
forward. Making this required means we never accrue a second wave
of opaque IDs while we're still cleaning up the first.
